Detect outlier thresholds via the IQR rule

Source: R/outliers.R
detect_outlier_thresholds.Rd

Computes lower and upper outlier bounds for a numeric variable using the IQR rule: Q1 - k*IQR and Q3 + k*IQR, with k = multiplier.

Usage

detect_outlier_thresholds(dataf, var_name, multiplier = 1.5, label = NULL)

Arguments

dataf

A data frame.

var_name

Name of a single numeric column in dataf.

multiplier

IQR multiplier. Default 1.5 (Tukey's classic threshold); use 3 for "extreme" outliers.

label

Optional label for the notes column. Defaults to var_name.

Value

A one-row data frame with columns LB (lower bound), UB (upper bound), and notes.

Examples

df <- data.frame(x = c(1:10, 100))
detect_outlier_thresholds(df, "x")
#>   LB UB notes
#> 1 -4 16     x
detect_outlier_thresholds(df, "x", multiplier = 3)
#>      LB   UB notes
#> 1 -11.5 23.5     x
